title: gamocarpha alpina subsp. alpina
intro: Gamocarpha alpina subsp. alpina, a subspecies of flowering plants in the family Asteraceae, is a shrub native to the high-altitude regions of the Andes mountains. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Peru and Chile, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Gamocarpha alpina subsp. alpina include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. The herbal preparations of Gamocarpha alpina subsp. alpina typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and stems, which are used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
